title: QA 4-tuple as a 4-node Y-structure causal DAG — pair invertibility and Pearl-level collapse
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that the A2 axiom (d=b+e, a=b+2e) IS the structural equation system of a 4-node causal DAG. The DAG has b,e exogenous and d,a endogenous colliders. Pair-invertibility theorem: all 6 pairs bijective on S_9 (gcd(2,9)=1); on S_24, 5 of 6 bijective with (b,a) 2-to-1 (gcd(2,24)=2). Pearl's three causal levels collapse because structural equations are deterministic.
source_attribution: Judea Pearl, 'Causality: Models, Reasoning, and Inference' (Cambridge University Press, 2nd ed. 2009) — structural causal models, do-calculus, three-level causal hierarchy. Sewall Wright, 'Correlation and Causation' (Journal of Agricultural Research 20, 1921, pp. 557-585) — original path analysis. QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-11. Prerequisites: families [191] Bateson Learning Levels, [150] Septenary Unit Group, [202] Aiq Bekar digital root.

causal_dag.topology: Y-structure (two exogenous sources, two endogenous collider sinks), acyclic, 4 nodes, 4 directed edges

pair_invertibility_theorem.statement: For modulus m, all 6 unordered pairs of {b,e,d,a} are bijective as maps from S_m = {1..m}^2 to their image iff gcd(2,m) = 1. When gcd(2,m) = g > 1, exactly the pair (b,a) is g-to-1.
pair_invertibility_theorem.proof_sketch: Given any 2 of (b,e,d,a), solve the 2x2 linear system from the A2 structural equations over Z/mZ. Pairs (b,e), (b,d), (e,d), (e,a), (d,a) have full-rank integer-matrix linear solves (determinant ±1). Pair (b,a) requires solving 2e = a - b (mod m), which has a unique solution iff 2 is invertible mod m.

pair_bijectivity_s9.note: S_9 has gcd(2,9)=1 so 2 is a unit; all 6 pairs yield 81/81 distinct projections

pair_bijectivity_s24.note: S_24 has gcd(2,24)=2 so 2 is NOT a unit; pair (b,a) has 288/576 distinct = 2-to-1 fold; other 5 pairs remain 576/576 bijective

pearl_level_collapse.reason: The A2 structural equations d=b+e, a=b+2e are deterministic integer arithmetic with NO stochastic noise term. Pearl's three levels (association, intervention, counterfactual) all deliver the same value because there is no noise to marginalize over. This is a degenerate but valid SCM.

witness_b1_e1.note: Levels 1 and 2 match exactly because d, a are deterministic functions of b, e. Level 3 is also deterministic given counterfactual b value.

theorem_nt_correspondence: The Pearl-level collapse under determinism is the SCM formulation of Theorem NT (observer projection firewall). QA causal structure lives entirely in the discrete A2 equations; the observer (measurement) layer sits orthogonally. Continuous functions that might correlate with the tuple are observer projections — they cannot be causal inputs because the SCM is closed under A2.

witnesses.note: Trivial: (b,e) are the exogenous inputs; uniquely determine d=b+e, a=b+2e.
The (d,a) pair is the 'observational' pair — when you only see the endogenous variables, you can still recover the exogenous drivers via linear algebra.
On S_9 the pair (b,a) IS bijective because 2 is a unit mod 9 (2^-1 = 5). 81/81 distinct.

witnesses.recovery: e = (a - b) / 2 has no unique solution: both e=2 and e=14 give b=1, a=5 after mod 24
witnesses.note: On S_24 the pair (b,a) is 2-to-1 because gcd(2,24)=2. Exactly 288/576 distinct projections. Witness: b=1, a=5 is attained by two distinct (b,e) states.

cross_references.relation: the 4-tuple hyperedge structure and this causal DAG are dual views of the same (b,e,d,a) object

verification_result: Y-structure is_acyclic=true; S_9 all 6 pairs bijective; S_24 pair (b,a) 2-to-1 others bijective; Pearl level 1/2 match for all 4 witness states; level 3 counterfactuals deterministic
